verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Arise, let us go hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I am the true Vine, (ye are the branches, ) and My Father is the Husbandman.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: ‘ Ignorance’ makes the soul timid and unmanly, just as instruction in heavenly doctrines makes it great and sublime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when it has enjoyed no care, it is in a manner timid, not by nature but by will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when I see the man who once was brave, now become a coward, I say that this latter feeling no longer belongs to nature, for what is natural is immutable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, when I see those who but now were cowards all at once become daring, I pass the same judgment, and refer all to will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since even the disciples were very fearful, before they had learned what they ought, and had been deemed worthy of the gift of the Spirit; yet afterwards they became bolder than lions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So Peter, who could not bear the threat of a damsel, was hung with his head downwards, and was scourged, and though he endured ten thousand dangers, would not be silent, but enduring what he endured as though it were a dream, in such a situation spake boldly; but not so before the Crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ said, “Arise, let us go hence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But why, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did he not know the hour at which Judas would come upon Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or perhaps He feared lest he should come and seize them, and lest the plotters should be upon him before he had furnished his most excellent teaching.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away with the thought! these things are far from His dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If then He did not fear, why did He remove them, and then after finish ing His discourse lead them into a garden known to Judas?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even had Judas come, could He not have blinded their eyes, as He also did when the traitor was not present?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did He remove them?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He alloweth the disciples a little breathing time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was likely that they, as being in a conspicuous place, would tremble and fear, both on the account of the time and the place, (for it was the depth of night,) and would not give heed to His words, but would be continually turning about, and imagining that they heard those who were to set upon them; and that more especially when their Master’s speech made them expect evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “yet a little while,” He saith, “and I am not with you,” and, “the ruler of this world cometh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since now when they heard these and the like words they were troubled, as though they should certainly be taken immediately, He leadeth them to another place, in order that thinking themselves in safety, they might listen to Him without fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they were about to hear lofty doctrines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “Arise, let us go hence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He addeth, and saith, “I am the Vine, ye are the branches.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What willeth He to imply by the comparison?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That the man who gives no heed to His words can have no life, and that the miracles about to take place, would be wrought by the power of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My Father is the Husbandman.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Doth the Son need a power working within?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away with the thought! this example does not signify this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe with what exactness He goeth through the comparison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith not that the “root” enjoys the care of the Husbandman, but, “the branches.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the foot is brought in in this place for no other purpose, but that they may learn that they can work nothing without His power, and that they ought to be united with Him by faith as the branch with the vine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Every branch in Me that beareth not fruit the Father taketh away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He alludeth to the manner of life, showing that without works it is not possible to be in Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And every branch that beareth fruit, He purgeth it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “causeth it to enjoy great care.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the root requires care rather than the branches, in being dug about, and cleared, yet about this He saith nothing here, but all about the branches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that He is sufficient to Himself, and that the disciples need much help from the Husbandman, although they be very excellent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “that which beareth fruit, He purgeth it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The one branch, because it is fruitless, cannot even remain in the Vine, but for the other, because it beareth fruit, He rendereth it more fruitful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This, some one might assert, was said with relation also to the persecutions then coming upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the “purgeth it,” is “pruneth,” which makes the branch bear better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence it is shown, that persecutions rather make men stronger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, lest they should ask concerning whom He said these things, and lest He should throw them back into anxiety, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He introduceth Himself as tending the branches?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have cleansed you,” He saith; yet above He declareth that the Father doth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But there is no separation between the Father and the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And now your part also must be performed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then to show that He did not this as needing their ministry, but for their advancement, He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself except it abide in the vine, so neither can he who abideth not in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that they might not be separated from Him by timidity, He fasteneth and glueth to Himself their souls slackened through fear, and holdeth out to them good hopes for the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the root remains, but to be taken away, or to be left, belongs to the branches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then having urged them on in both ways, by things pleasant and things painful, He requireth first what is to be done on our side.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that abideth in Me, and I in him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the Son contributeth not less than the Father towards the care of the disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Father purgeth, but He keepeth them in Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The abiding in the root is that which maketh the branches to be fruit-bearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that which is not purged, if it remain on the root, bears fruit, though perhaps not so much as it ought; but that which remains not, bears none at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But still the “purging” also hath been shown to belong to the Son, and the “abiding in the root,” to the Father, who also begat the Root.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how all is common, both the “purging,” and the enjoying the virtue which is from the root?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now it were a great penalty, the being able to do nothing, but He stayeth not the punishment at this point, but carrieth on His discourse farther.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He is cast forth,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No longer enjoying the benefit of the husbandman’s hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And is withered.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, if he had aught of the root, he loses it; if any grace, he is stripped of this, and is bereft of the help and life which proceed from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what the end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He is cast into the fire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not such he who abideth with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He showeth what it is to “abide,” and saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If My words abide in you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that with reason I said above, that He seeketh the proof by works?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He had said, “Whatsoever ye shall ask I will do it” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14, 15 ), He added, “If ye love Me, ye will keep My commandments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here, “If ye abide in Me, and My words abide in you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said to show that they who plotted against Him should be burnt up, but that “they” should bear fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then transferring the fear from them to the others, and showing that they should be invincible, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Herein is My Father glorified, that ye be My disciples, and bear much fruit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence He maketh His discourse credible, for if the bearing fruit pertains to the glory of the Father, He will not neglect His own glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And ye shall be My disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how he that beareth fruit, he is the disciple?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “In this is the Father glorified”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He rejoiceth when ye abide in Me, when ye bear fruit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father hath loved Me, so have I loved you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here at length He speaketh in a more human manner, for this, as spoken to men, has its peculiar force.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since what a measure of love did He manifest, who chose to die, who counted worthy of such honor those who were His slaves, His haters, His open enemies, and led them up to the heavens!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If then I love you, be bold; if it be the glory of My Father that ye bear fruit, imagine nothing ill.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then that He may not make them supine, observe how He braceth them again,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Continue ye in My love.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For this ye have the power to do.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how shall this be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye keep My commandments, even as I have kept my Father’s commandments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, His discourse proceedeth in a human way; for certainly the Lawgiver would not be subject to commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that here also, as I am always saying, this is declared because of the infirmity of the hearers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He chiefly speaketh to their suspicions, and by every means showeth them that they are in safety, and that their enemies are being lost, and that all, whatever they have, they have from the Son, and that, if they show forth a pure life, none shall ever have the mastery over them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe that He discourseth with them in a very authoritative manner, for He said not, “abide in the love of My Father,” but, “in Mine”; then, lest they should say, “when Thou hast set us at war with all men, Thou leavest us, and departest,” He showeth that He doth not leave them, but is so joined to them if they will, as the branch in the vine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, lest from confidence they should become supine, He saith not that the blessing cannot be removed if they are slack-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in order not to refer the action to Himself, and so make them more apt to fall, He saith, “Herein is My Father glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For everywhere He manifesteth His own and His Father’s love towards them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not the things of the Jews, then, were “glory,” but those which they were about to receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that they might not say, “we have been driven from the possessions of our fathers, we have been deserted, we have become naked, and destitute of all things,” “Look,” He saith, “on Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I am loved by the Father, yet still I suffer these things appointed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so I am not now leaving you because I love you not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if I am slain, and take not this for a proof of not being loved by the Father, neither ought ye to be troubled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, if ye continue in My love, these dangers shall not be able to do you any mischief on the score of love.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then love is a thing mighty and irresistible, not a bare word, let us manifest it by our actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He reconciled us when we were His enemies, let us, now that we have become His friends, remain so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He led the way, let us at least follow; He loveth us not for His own advantage, (for He needeth nothing,) let us at least love Him for our profit; He loved us being His enemies, let us at least love Him being our friend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At present we do the contrary; for every day God is blasphemed through us, through our plunderings, through our covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And perhaps one of you will say, “Every day thy discourse is about covetousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Would that I could speak about it every night too; would that I could do so, following you about in the market-place, and at your table; would that both wives, and friends, and children, and domestics, and tillers of the soil, and neighbors, and the very pavement and walls, could ever shout forth this word, that so we might perchance have relaxed a little.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this malady hath seized upon all the world, and occupies the souls of all, and great is the tyranny of Mammon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We have been ransomed by Christ, and are the slaves of gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We proclaim the sovereignty of the one, and obey the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whatever “he” commands we readily obey, and we have refused to know family, or friendship, or nature, or laws, or anything, for him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No one looks up to Heaven, no one thinks about things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But there will be a time, when there will be no profit even in these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the grave,” it saith, “who shall confess to Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Gold is a desirable thing, and procures us much luxury, and makes us to be honored, but not in like manner as doth Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For from the wealthy man many even turn aside, and hate him, but him who lives virtuously they respect and honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But” saith some one “the poor man is derided, even though he be virtuous.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not among men, but brutes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore he ought not so much as to notice them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if asses were to bray and daws chatter at us, while all wise men commended us, we should not, losing sight of this latter audience, have regard to clamors of the brutes; for like to daws, and worse than asses, are they who admire present things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, if an earthly king approve thee, thou makest no account of the many, though they all deride thee; but if the Lord of the universe praise thee, seekest thou the good words of beetles and gnats?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is what these men are, compared with God, or rather not even this, but something viler, if there be aught such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long do we wallow in the mire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long do we set sluggards and belly-gods for our judges?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They can prove dicers well, drunkards, those who live for the belly, but as for virtue and vice, they cannot imagine so much as a dream.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any one taunt thee because thou hast not skill to draw the channels of the watercourses, thou wilt not think it any terrible thing, but wilt even laugh at him who objects to thee ignorance of this kind; and dost thou, when thou desirest to practice virtue, appoint as judges those who know nothing of it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account we never reach that art.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We commit our case not to the practiced, but to the unlearned, and they judge not according to the rules of art, but according to their own ignorance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, I exhort you, let us despise the many; or rather let us desire neither praises, nor possessions, nor wealth, nor deem poverty any evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For poverty is to us a teacher of prudence, and endurance, and all true wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus Lazarus lived in poverty, and received a crown; Jacob desired to get bread only; and Joseph was in the extreme of poverty, being not merely a slave, but also a prisoner; and on this account we admire him the more, and we do not so much praise him when he distributed the corn, as when he dwelt in the dungeon: not when he wore the diadem, but when the chain; not when he sat upon the throne, but when he was plotted against and sold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Considering then all these things, and the crowns twined for us after the conflicts, let us admire not wealth, and honor, and luxury, and power, but poverty, and the chain, and bonds, and endurance in the cause of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the end of those things is full of troubles and confusion, and their lot is bound up with this present life; but the fruit of these, heaven, and the good things in the heavens, which neither eye hath seen, nor ear heard; which may we all obtain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever.
